questions.question: Prove that the set of positive integers can be well-ordered using the standard ordering. What property of the ordering is used to establish the well-ordering?
questions.hint: Define what it means to have a well-ordering and how the standard ordering of the integers establishes this property

questions.question: Using mathematical induction, prove that the sum of the first n positive integers is given by n(n+1)/2. What is the basis step of the induction proof?
questions.hint: Define the statement to be proven and define the basis step of the induction proof

questions.question: A set S is known to be well-ordered using the standard ordering. Prove that if T is a subset of S and T is also well-ordered using the standard ordering, then T can be put into a one-to-one correspondence with a subset of S. What property of the ordering is used to establish this correspondence?
questions.hint: Define what it means for a set to be put into a one-to-one correspondence and how the well-ordering of S and T establishes this correspondence
